onboarding.title: Welcome to Flocks
onboarding.description: Rex will guide you through initial setup via conversation — about 5-10 min, skip any step.
onboarding.presetUser: Hi Rex! I just started using Flocks — please help me with the initial setup.
onboarding.presetRex:
Hello! I'm Rex, Flocks' AI security operations assistant 👋

Great to meet you! I'll guide you through the initial setup — connecting security APIs, IM channels, NDR devices and more. It takes about 5–10 minutes and you can skip any step.

Ready? Let's get started 🚀
onboarding.initialMessage: Please start the onboarding process. After any IM channel is configured, explicitly guide me to open the corresponding channel in the Channels page, click "Restart Connection", click "Save" if there are any page changes, and continue only after the connection is restored.

bootstrap.title: A default model is required
bootstrap.subtitle: Set up a default model first, then start chatting. If you choose a ThreatBook free model, Flocks will also configure the matching ThreatBook API and MCP services for you.
bootstrap.welcomeIntro: Before you begin, you need to configure a default model. Once validation succeeds, you can start chatting right away and connect more services later.
bootstrap.welcomeHint: ThreatBook offers free models for both Chinese and Global users. Chinese users can also enable ThreatBook MCP and API services together.
bootstrap.primaryThreatBookCnFreeHint: ThreatBook provides a free model plus Threatbook MCP / API services for Chinese users.
bootstrap.primaryThreatBookGlobalFreeHint: ThreatBook provides a free model plus ThreatBook API service for Global users.
bootstrap.primaryThreatBookCnHint: After validation, Flocks will also configure the ThreatBook-China model, API, and MCP services.
bootstrap.primaryThreatBookGlobalHint: After validation, Flocks will also configure the ThreatBook Global model and API service.
bootstrap.primaryThreatBookCnSuccess: Model verification succeeded. ThreatBook-China API and MCP have also been configured.
bootstrap.primaryThreatBookGlobalSuccess: Model verification succeeded. ThreatBook Global API has also been configured.
bootstrap.primaryThirdPartySuccess: Default model verification succeeded. {{provider}} / {{model}} has been configured. You can optionally enable ThreatBook services below.
bootstrap.configuredThreatBookCnDetails: Your default model is already set to the ThreatBook-China free model. You can start right away, or open edit mode to change and re-verify it.
bootstrap.configuredThreatBookGlobalDetails: Your default model is already set to the ThreatBook Global free model. You can start right away, or open edit mode to change and re-verify it.
bootstrap.configuredThirdPartyDetails: Your default model is already configured. You can start right away, or open edit mode to change and re-verify it.
bootstrap.optionalThreatBookSubtitle: Even if you use another model as the default, you can still add ThreatBook API here. Chinese users can also enable MCP at the same time.
bootstrap.optionalThreatBookCnSuccess: ThreatBook-China services verified successfully. API and MCP have both been configured.
bootstrap.optionalThreatBookGlobalSuccess: ThreatBook Global services verified successfully. API has been configured.
bootstrap.testFailed: Model call failed — please check your API key
bootstrap.serviceTestFailed: Service verification failed — please check the API key and selected region
bootstrap.startBlockedHint: Please finish configuring and verifying a default model first
bootstrap.configured: System default model configured — ready to start
bootstrap.saveError: Failed to save, please try again
bootstrap.startError: Failed to start a session, please try again

modelsPageHint.link: Go to Models page for more

bootstrap.regionSubtitle: ThreatBook activation links, valid keys, and service presets depend on the selected region.
bootstrap.modelStrategySubtitle: Use the free ThreatBook model, or keep another model provider as your default.
bootstrap.useThreatBookModelHint: ThreatBook will be set as the default LLM, and ThreatBook API plus Chinese MCP will be configured together.
bootstrap.useOtherModelHint: Configure a third-party default model, and optionally add a ThreatBook key for extra services.
bootstrap.tbRequiredHint: ThreatBook is selected as the default model, so a region-matching ThreatBook key is required here.
bootstrap.tbOptionalHint: Recommended. Even with another default model, this can still enable ThreatBook API and Chinese MCP.
bootstrap.cnResourcesRequired: For Chinese, this will configure: ThreatBook model + ThreatBook API + ThreatBook MCP.
bootstrap.cnResourcesOptional: For Chinese, this can additionally enable: ThreatBook model + ThreatBook API + ThreatBook MCP.
bootstrap.globalResourcesRequired: For International, this will configure: ThreatBook model + ThreatBook API.
bootstrap.globalResourcesOptional: For International, this can additionally enable: ThreatBook model + ThreatBook API.
bootstrap.tbSkipHint: This step is optional. You can skip it and still start chatting, but ThreatBook model, MCP, and API services will remain disabled for now.
bootstrap.thirdPartySubtitle: Choose another provider, enter its key, and select the default model.
bootstrap.thirdPartyKeyPlaceholder: Paste the third-party model API key
bootstrap.thirdPartyBaseUrlPlaceholder: Enter the model service base URL if needed
bootstrap.thirdPartyModelIdPlaceholder: Enter the model ID (e.g. gpt-4o, deepseek-chat)
bootstrap.thirdPartyModelHint: Will verify and set this as the default model: {{model}}
bootstrap.thirdPartyAdvancedHint: For advanced custom setups such as OpenAI Compatible, use the Models page instead.

hookStatus.title: Hook System
hookStatus.autoSaveDesc: When you create a new session (similar to /new command), the previous session's conversation will be automatically saved to the memory system.
